title: OpenClaw upgrade should not fail on a missing bundled channel setup entry

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62867.

task.summary: Diagnose why an upgrade leaves the CLI dead on startup because a bundled plugin entry path is missing.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm that the upgrade regression is coming from a missing bundled entry path, localize it to packaged plugin bootstrap assets, and propose an ops-level repair plan that restores startup.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: After upgrading to 2026.4.7, every OpenClaw command fails because the Telegram bundled setup entry cannot be found.
error_logs: Failed to read config: bundled plugin entry './src/channel.setup.js' failed to open
ENOENT: no such file or directory, lstat .../dist/extensions/telegram/src/channel.setup.js
reproduction_steps: Inspect the startup error and packaged entry path evidence.
Explain what packaging or bootstrap path must be corrected so the CLI can start again.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models an upgrade and packaging regression, not a code-patch task.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic startup regression derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62867.
